bezeichnung: Game Theory and Artificial Intelligence in Cyber Security
lernziele:
Recent cyber and supply chain attacks on the Swiss federal administration and various Swiss companies have shown that cyber security is becoming increasingly important in daily life.
In cybersecurity, Game Theory can be used to begin understanding what a potential attacker may be attempting to do based on the payoffs for their actions. This better understanding can help to improve the decision-making of an organization when it comes to IT Security problems.
In addition, many logs are generated in computer networks. For example, every network packet that passes through a firewall is logged. This large number of log files is suitable for analysis using big data approaches and machine learning. By using machine learning, patterns in the network data can be found that may indicate attacks

 

The students….

  • will be able to didactically prepare and present the main tasks and challenges of cybersecurity, the concepts of game theory and their application in cybersecurity, the methods and frameworks of emulation of such systems for a broad audience of computer professionals.
  • learn the theory and application of hacking frameworks
  • understand the key concepts of GameTheory for Cybersecurity
  • understand the key concepts of Machine Learning for Cybersecurity

kurse.bezeichnung: Game Theory and Artificial Intelligence in Cyber Security
kurse.lernziele:
The students….

  • will be able to didactically prepare and present the main tasks and challenges of cybersecurity, the concepts of game theory and their application in cybersecurity, the methods and frameworks of emulation of such systems for a broad audience of computer professionals.
  • learn the theory and application of hacking frameworks
  • understand the key concepts of GameTheory for Cybersecurity
  • understand the key concepts of Machine Learning for Cybersecurity
kurse.planinhalt:
• Fundamentals of Computer Networks
  • Fundamentals of Encryption
  • Basic knowledge of Cyber Security and hacking Frameworks
  • Understanding of Network Data Visualization
  • Introduction and application of Game Theory for Cyber Security Problems
  • Introduction and application of Machine Learning for Cyber Security
kurse.bibliographie:
[1.] Charles A. Kamhoua, Christopher D. Kiekintveld, Fei Fang, Quanyan Zhu: Game Theory and Machine Learning for Cyber Security, November 2021, ISBN: 978-1-119-72392-9
[2.] Chong Li, Meikang Qiu, Reinforcement Learning for Cyber-Physical Systems, Februar 2019, ISBN-13 : 978-1138543539
[3.] Tansu Alpcan, Tamer Başar, Network Security: A Decision and Game-Theoretic Approach, Network Security (cambridge.org), December 2010 ,ISBN-13 : 978-0521119320
[4.] Rüdiger Schreiner, COMPUTERNETZWERKE: Von den Grundlagen zur Funktion und Anwendung, Edition: 7., ISBN: 978-3-446-46005-8
[5.] Andrew S. Tanenbaum, David J. Wetherall, Computernetzwerke, 5., aktualisierte Auflage, ISBN: 978-3-86894-137-1
[6.] Stephan Spitz , Michael Pramateftakis , Joachim Swoboda, Kryptographie und IT-Sicherheit, https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-3-8348-8120-5 , 2011, ISBN: 978-3-8348-8120-5

[7.] Seth James Nielson , Christopher K. Monson, Practical Cryptography in Python: Learning Correct Cryptography by Example, 2019, ISBN: 978-1-4842-4900-0
[8.] William Stallings, Network Security Essentials: Applications and Standards, 6th edition, ISBN-13: 9780137561650
[9]. William Cheswick, Steven Bellovin, Aviel Rubin, John Fuller, Firewalls and Internet Security: Repelling the Wily Hacker, 2nd Edition, ISBN-13: 978-0201634662
[10.] Tim Philipp Schäfers, Hacking im Web, 2. Auflage, ISBN: 978-3-645-20636-5
[11.] Michael Messner, Hacking mit Metasploit: Das umfassende Handbuch zu Penetration Testing und Metasploit, November 2017, ISBN: 978-3-86490-523-0
[12.] Eric Amberg und Daniel Schmid, Hacking: Der umfassende Praxis-Guide, 2. Auflage 2022, ISBN: 9783747504826
[13.] Jay Jacobs, Bob Rudis, Data-Driven Security : Analysis, Visualization and Dashboards, February 2014, ISBN-13: 978-1118793725
[14.] Greg Conti, Security Data Visualization : Graphical Techniques for Network Analysis, September 2007, ISBN-13: 978-1593271435
kurse.empfLehrmittel:
• Self-study with supervision
  • Literature research
  • Problem-based learning,
  • Documentation of theory in 12 lessons as follows:
    - V01 The Fundamentals of Networking
    - V02 Fundamentals of Encryption
    - V03 Introduction to CyberSecurity
    - V04 Hacking of Web Applications
    - V05 Penetration testing
    - V06 Security Data Visualization
    - V07 Introduction to Game Theory
    - V08 Game theoretic models for CyberSecurity
    - V09 Introduction to Machine Learning for CyberSecurity
    - V10 Adversal Machine Learning
    - V11 Introduction to Reinforcement Learning
    - V112 Reinforcement Learning in CyberSecurity
  • Define what should be done in the associated labs without elaborating them.
kurse.leistungsnachweis: Deliverable: Didactically successful introduction to "AI for Cybersecurity" in the form of 12 Lessons and a brief overview of the corresponding Lab without elaborating the Labs in detail.
